pattern: Try matching the smaller tree against the subtree rooted at every node of the larger one, where a match means identical structure and identical values all the way down.
hints: Break it into two questions: at a given node, do two trees match exactly, and where do you anchor that check.
Write a helper that returns whether two trees are identical, then apply it starting from each node of the big tree.
Recurse through the big tree; at each node test for an exact match against the target, and answer yes if any anchor point matches.

edge_inputs.description: subtree appears as a left branch
values match but an extra descendant breaks the structure
both single nodes with the same value
the whole tree equals the target
matching value appears but its children differ
repeated value where only one occurrence is a real match

stress.description: build a random tree, then sometimes use an actual subtree as the target and sometimes a random one
stress.note: mixing genuine subtrees and random targets exercises both outcomes
